page.text: Pollution in general refers to the pollution of air, water or soil by material that is harmful to life on earth, namely all plants, animals and human beings. There are different kinds of pollution caused either by nature (e.g. volcanic eruptions) or by man (e.g. oil spills and industrial waste in sewer pipes). We can categorise pollution as follows - light pollution: extreme light emission due to modern city life - noise pollution: loud harmful or annoying continuous noise - thermal pollution: heat from hot water discharged by industry into rivers, lakes or seas, killing or endangering the marine environment. In the following texts we will concentrate on marine pollution namely chemicals or particles which enter the water and have a negative or even harmful effect on marine life.
